final-file-room-capstone.label: Final File Room Capstone
final-file-room-capstone.summary: Clean up a real-looking event workspace and leave it ready for the next person.

sc-capstone-first-check.title: What is the best first move in a messy folder?
sc-capstone-first-check.prompt: Start by looking before changing anything.
sc-capstone-first-check.hint: Check where you are and what files are present.

help.concept: Safe terminal work starts with inspection.
help.hint_1: Use `pwd` and `ls` before moving or deleting.
help.hint_2: Choose the action that gives you information first.

options.b: Run `pwd` and `ls` to inspect the workspace
options.c: Rename every file to shorter names
options.d: Leave the folder without checking it

sc-capstone-copy-command.title: Which command makes a backup copy?
sc-capstone-copy-command.prompt: A backup should leave the original in place.

help.concept: `cp` creates another copy of a file.
help.hint_1: The original should still exist after the command.
help.hint_2: Choose the command used for copying.

sc-capstone-remove-temp.title: Which command removes the old temp file?
sc-capstone-remove-temp.prompt: Remove only the unwanted temp file.

help.hint_1: Use the exact path so you do not delete the wrong file.
help.hint_2: Choose the command that removes one file.

mc-capstone-safe-handoff.title: Choose the safe handoff habits.
mc-capstone-safe-handoff.prompt: Pick the habits that make terminal cleanup reliable.
mc-capstone-safe-handoff.hint: Think about checking, sorting, and backing up.

help.concept: A good handoff is organized and easy to verify.
help.hint_1: Do not choose a broad delete habit.
help.hint_2: Choose habits that keep important work findable.

options.b: Back up an important note before finishing
options.c: Delete whole folders without checking names
options.d: Create a ready marker after the cleanup is complete

ci-capstone-create-map.title: Create the event room folders
ci-capstone-create-map.prompt: The community center needs a clean file room. Create `event-room/notes`, `event-room/scripts`, `event-room/archive`, and `backups` while keeping the incoming files available.
ci-capstone-create-map.hint: Create destination folders before moving the incoming files.

help.concept: A clean folder map gives each file a clear destination.
help.hint_2: Do not remove the incoming files in this first step.

ci-capstone-sort-files.title: Sort the incoming files
ci-capstone-sort-files.prompt: Move `agenda.txt` to notes, `setup.sh` to scripts, and rename `guest-list.txt` as `event-room/archive/guests.txt`.
ci-capstone-sort-files.hint: Use `mv` to move files and to rename the guest list.

help.concept: `mv` can move a file to another folder or give it a new name.
help.hint_1: Create the destination folders first if needed.
help.hint_2: The incoming copies should be gone after the move.

ci-capstone-finish-handoff.title: Finish the handoff
ci-capstone-finish-handoff.prompt: Read the agenda, back it up to `backups/agenda-backup.txt`, remove `event-room/incoming/old.tmp`, and create `event-room/ready.txt`.

help.concept: A final handoff keeps important files, removes clutter, and leaves a clear ready marker.
help.hint_1: Back up the agenda before submitting.
help.hint_2: Check that the temp file is gone and `ready.txt` exists.

try-capstone-make-warmup-folders.title: Prepare a warmup room
try-capstone-make-warmup-folders.hint: Use `mkdir -p` with all three folder paths.

help.concept: Final cleanup starts by preparing destination folders.
help.hint_1: Use `mkdir -p` with all three folder paths.
help.hint_2: Run the command in the terminal, check the response, then submit.

try-capstone-sort-one-file.title: Sort a sample guest list
try-capstone-sort-one-file.hint: Make the destination folder if needed, then use `mv`.

help.concept: Put files where the next person will expect to find them.
help.hint_1: Make the destination folder if needed, then use `mv`.
help.hint_2: Run the command in the terminal, check the response, then submit.

help.concept: Leave an extra copy of important handoff notes.
help.hint_2: Run the command in the terminal, check the response, then submit.

file-room-scenario.title: The file room handoff
file-room-scenario.bodyMarkdown:
A community center just finished planning an event. The workspace has an `incoming` folder with notes, a small setup script, a guest list, and an old temporary file.

Your job is to leave a clean file room for the next volunteer. You will create folders, move files into the right places, make one backup, and remove the temporary file.

sort-the-inbox.title: Sort the incoming files
sort-the-inbox.bodyMarkdown:
Use names that make the handoff obvious. Agenda notes belong in `notes`, setup commands belong in `scripts`, and the guest list belongs in `archive`.

`mv` is the main tool here. It can move a file and rename it at the same time.

handoff-check.title: Check the handoff
handoff-check.bodyMarkdown:
Before finishing, read the agenda, make a backup, remove only the old temp file, and leave a `ready.txt` marker.

That final marker tells the next person the workspace has been checked.
